lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Good morning. May I see your passport and booking confirmation?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Sure, here you go.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Would you like a window or aisle seat?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Window seat, please.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Do you have any checked luggage?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Yes, one suitcase.

lines.speaker: Agent
lines.text: Here's your boarding pass. Gate B12. Boarding starts at 9:30.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Please place your bags on the belt and remove any laptops.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Do I need to take off my shoes?

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Yes, and please remove your belt too.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Okay, here you go.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: Please step through the scanner.

lines.speaker: Officer
lines.text: You're all clear. Have a nice flight!

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: Attention passengers on Flight BA247 to New York. We regret to inform you that your flight has been delayed by approximately two hours due to weather conditions.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Excuse me, I have a connecting flight in New York. Will I still make it?

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: What time is your connecting flight?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: It departs at 8:15 PM local time.

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: With the delay, you would arrive at 7:45 PM. That's very tight. I'd recommend we rebook your connection to be safe.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: That would be great. Can you find me a later flight to Chicago?

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: There's a 10:30 PM departure to Chicago. I can put you on that at no extra charge since this is an airline-caused delay.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Yes, please book that one. Will I receive a meal voucher for the wait?

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: Absolutely. Here's a $15 voucher valid at any restaurant in Terminal 3. I apologize for the inconvenience.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Thank you for your help. Where can I charge my phone while I wait?

lines.speaker: Gate Agent
lines.text: There are charging stations near Gate B8 and also in the lounge area across from the bookshop.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Perfect. I appreciate your assistance.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Next, please. Good afternoon. Passport, please.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Here you are.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: What is the purpose of your visit?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: I'm here on business. I have meetings with a client in London.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: How long do you plan to stay in the United Kingdom?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Five days. I'm flying back on Friday.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Do you have a return ticket?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Yes, here's my return booking confirmation.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Where will you be staying during your visit?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: At the Hilton Hotel in central London. I have the reservation printed here.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Are you carrying more than ten thousand pounds in cash or equivalent?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: No, I only have about two hundred pounds in cash.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Do you have anything to declare — food, plants, or agricultural products?

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: No, nothing to declare.

lines.speaker: Immigration Officer
lines.text: Very well. Welcome to the United Kingdom. Enjoy your stay.

lines.speaker: Traveler
lines.text: Thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Excuse me, I've been waiting at the carousel for thirty minutes and my suitcase hasn't appeared.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Can I see your baggage claim tag? It should be attached to your boarding pass.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Yes, here it is. Tag number LH-482917.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: Thank you. Let me check the system. Which flight did you arrive on?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Flight LH 1042 from Frankfurt. It landed about forty-five minutes ago.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: I can see your bag was scanned in Frankfurt but doesn't appear to have been loaded onto your flight. It may arrive on the next flight at 6:15 PM.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: That's really frustrating. I have important documents and medication in that suitcase.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: I completely understand. Let me file a delayed baggage report. Could you describe the suitcase for me?

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: It's a large black hard-shell suitcase with a red ribbon tied to the handle.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: I've noted that. Can I have your local address and phone number? We'll deliver the bag to you free of charge once it arrives.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: I'm staying at the Marriott on Park Avenue. My number is 555-0192.

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: In the meantime, you're entitled to a toiletries kit and up to $50 reimbursement for essential purchases. Here's the claim form and your reference number.

lines.speaker: Passenger
lines.text: Thank you. How soon will I hear back about the bag?

lines.speaker: Baggage Agent
lines.text: We'll call you within four hours with an update. You can also track the status online using that reference number.
